# @zakkster/lite-headless / badge

Count indicator + dot variant. Small decoration typically anchored to
another element (button, avatar, menu item).

## Quick example

```js
import { createBadge } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/badge";

const b = createBadge({
    count: 3,
    max: 99,          // count > max paints "99+"
    intent: "danger",
});
b.attachRoot(badgeEl);

b.setCount(5);        // reactive
b.increment();        // → 6
b.reset();            // → 0; hides (data-hidden) unless showZero: true
```

Dot mode:
```js
createBadge({ dot: true, intent: "success" });
```

## Options

| Option | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `count` | `0` | Initial count |
| `max` | `99` | Counts over this show "max+" (e.g., "99+") |
| `dot` | `false` | Dot mode (no number, just a dot) |
| `showZero` | `false` | If false, hides when count === 0 |
| `intent` | `"default"` | `default \| primary \| success \| info \| warning \| danger` |

## API

```ts
{
    count(): number
    displayed(): string       // "5", "99+", etc.
    isDot: boolean            // readonly
    max: number               // readonly
    intent: string            // readonly

    setCount(n: number): void
    increment(by?: number): void
    decrement(by?: number): void
    reset(): void

    attachRoot(el): off

    destroy(): void
}
```

## Painted attributes

- `data-badge-root` — slot marker
- `data-intent="..."` — intent color
- `data-dot` — boolean, dot mode
- `data-count="<displayed>"` — count mode only; shows e.g. "3" or "99+"
- `data-hidden` + `hidden` — when count === 0 and `showZero === false`
- `aria-label` — auto-generated ("3 items", "Indicator") only if not pre-set

## Wrapper (`<lite-badge>`)

```html
<lite-badge count="3" intent="danger">3</lite-badge>
<lite-badge dot intent="success"></lite-badge>
<lite-badge count="105" max="99" intent="primary"></lite-badge>
```

Reactive: `count` attribute flows through bidirectionally.
Host methods: `setCount(n)`, `increment()`, `decrement()`, `reset()`.

## Composition

Typical mounting pattern: position a `<lite-badge>` absolutely over
the corner of a parent element.

```html
<button class="bell-wrap">
    🔔
    <lite-badge count="3" intent="danger" class="bell-badge">3</lite-badge>
</button>
```

```css
.bell-wrap { position: relative; }
.bell-badge { position: absolute; top: -4px; right: -4px; }
```
